TextENGNew: I woke up.
...My memories of what followed were a jumbled mess. What happened after that, how I made it back to this room...@k I had to cradle my head in both arms just to piece it together.
changeReason: Old says memories were 'missing' while Japanese (ぐちゃぐちゃ) and new say 'jumbled mess'; old also omits the detail about needing to hold her head to remember.

TextENGNew: My left hand was wrapped in bandages.
The three fingers on my left hand where the nails had been torn off... I did not want to look at them. Perhaps it was the anesthesia, but they had a strange, alien feel to them.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ...After that, I had three fingernails ripped from me... and the matter was considered settled, more or less.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: Once the old hag had witnessed it, she had droned on at length about something or other in that self-important way of hers, but none of it had stuck.
After that, I was immediately driven to an orthopedic clinic in Okinomiya.
changeReason: Medical facility type differs: 'hospital' vs 'orthopedic clinic'.

TextENGNew: The jostling of the car on the way there was absolutely unbearable.
...Someone told me headache pills would work as painkillers and handed me a sheet of twelve tablets... I swallowed them to dull the pain.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: The pills really did work as fast as they claimed on TV.
But the effect wore off after only about ten minutes.@k ...Without even water, I endured by swallowing pills dry with my own saliva every time the pain came surging back.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ...While I was being treated at the hospital and sitting in a daze in the waiting room...
......Kasai had come.
Kasai had probably wanted to ask if I was all right.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: But it was plain to see that I was anything but, with my left hand swaddled in bandages and suspended in a sling.
Kasai looked completely unharmed.
...At least, as far as I could see, there was not a single bruise on him.
changeReason: Old translation appears significantly truncated compared to new translation and Japanese source.

TextENGNew: ......Seeing that, I knew my sacrifice had meant something.
Kasai said nothing.
...Expressionless, standing close at my side... I could not even tell whether he wanted to say something to me or not.
The slight pause before Kasai answered told me... he was probably angry.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...I did not regret having revealed my true identity to protect Satoshi-kun back then.
But... when I thought about the trouble I had caused Kasai and Uncle Yoshiroh... my feelings were a tangled mess.
......Do you know what happened to Uncle Yoshiroh...?
No.
......He is a resilient man.@k@vS06/15/151500155. I believe he is fine.@k@vS06/15/151500156. You should apologize to him when you get the chance.
...I had wanted to ask about Satoshi-kun next.
I worried whether he had been caught up in the fallout from my actions -- seized by the main family, or worse, subjected to something terrible.
changeReason: Character name 'Sonozaki' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: But... I hesitated to bring up Satoshi-kun's name in front of Kasai.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: In the end, I had weighed Satoshi-kun against them... and I had chosen to disregard them.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ...Was Satoshi-kun safe?
Even if they had not harmed him directly... I could only hope they had not captured him and locked him away somewhere...
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ......After paying the bill, Kasai drove me back to my apartment...
...My foggy head finally registered that someone was knocking on my door.
Shion-san... it is time to get up.@k@vS06/15/151500158. Staying in bed this long is not good for you.
changeReason: Old omits the instruction to get up (体を起こして); new includes 'it is time to get up' present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: It was Kasai.
Kasai had bought a boxed meal and some side dishes. We spread them out on the table... and picked at them together.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: "......Are you feeling any calmer?"
changeReason: Old asks 'How do you feel?' while Japanese (落ち着かれましたか) and new specifically ask 'Are you feeling any calmer?' — different questions.

TextENGNew: ...I suppose "calmer" is one word for it. More like... deflated.@k@vS06/06/150601015. ...Haah...
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: Have you been lying in bed these past few days?@k@vS06/15/151500161. Have you been eating?
changeReason: Old omits 'these past few days' (この数日間) and 'lying in bed' (伏せっていた) present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...I have been eating whatever was left in the fridge.@k@vS06/06/150601017. Other than that, I have just been either staring at the ceiling or sleeping.
Three days had passed since then.@k ...I had spent almost the entire time lying down.
My sleep had been shallow, and I had half-woken several times, but I never had the energy to actually get up... I just drifted in that shallow, thoughtless half-sleep.
I had forgotten about hunger until now, but the smell of the boxed meal reminded me of it.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: I had nothing to say... so I just silently lifted my chopsticks to my mouth.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Yesterday... I received a call from Mion-san.@k@vS06/15/151500163. She said she had tried calling your room several times but could not get through.
The mention of Mion's name... it felt like a door I had finally managed to close was being pried open again.
But I did not interrupt, and let Kasai continue.

"......The Sonozaki main family has decided... that since you settled the matter yourself, they consider it closed.@k@vS06/15/151500165. ...Your withdrawal from the academy will be submitted.@k@vS06/15/151500166. You will be transferred to a school in Okinomiya."
changeReason: Character name 'Sonozaki' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: "...........So Sonozaki Shion's right to exist is finally being acknowledged, huh..."
changeReason: Character name 'Shion' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: "...That is correct.@k@vS06/15/151500168. From now on, you are free to live as Sonozaki Shion."
changeReason: Character name 'Shion' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...However, you must not go near Hinamizawa without good reason.@k@vS06/15/151500170. You must not impersonate Sonozaki Mion.@k@vS06/15/151500171. You must not show yourself in public carelessly... there are other stipulations as well, but in essence...
"So they will acknowledge my existence... as long as I never do anything to embarrass the main family again."
changeReason: Old says 'they don't want me to hang around' while Japanese and new say 'never do anything to embarrass the main family again' — substantially different conditions.

TextENGNew: That is correct.@k@vS06/15/151500173. ...Of course, this is not permanent.@k@vS06/15/151500174. It is only until things cool down.
There is no specific deadline, but... I think it would be wise to continue living inconspicuously, as you have been, at least through the end of the year.
...Living quietly in Okinomiya is more than enough for me.@k@vS06/06/150601021. ...That is how I have been living all along, after all.
Kasai gave a bitter smile.
I returned the same kind of smile.
And then... without breaking the flow of conversation... I voiced the question I had been wanting to ask all along.
changeReason: Old says 'before the conversation ended' implying urgency while Japanese (流れを断ち切らず) and new say 'without breaking the flow' implying deliberate seamlessness.

TextENGNew: ..........Satoshi-kun... what happened to him...?
I wanted him to just say one word: "He is safe."
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: But Kasai did not give me that answer.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: "...Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150601024. ...Is Satoshi-kun... safe...?"
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ......Shion-san.
Kasai set his chopsticks down.
"The main family... has instructed me to tell you to forget about Houjou Satoshi."
changeReason: Character name 'Houjou' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ".....................What did you just say?"
changeReason: Old translates as 'What?' while Japanese (何それ) and new translate as 'What did you just say?' — different intensity of response.

TextENGNew: Shion-san.@k@vS06/15/151500180. ...I do not wish to quarrel with you, so I would rather not discuss this further.@k@vS06/15/151500181. ...I am only relaying what the main family has told me.
Wh-... What do you mean you do not want to discuss it...?!@k@vS06/06/150601027. Kasai!
Kasai acted as though he had not heard me and resumed eating.
...I had never seen Kasai be this stubborn and cold.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...The Sonozaki family had been using the Houjou family as a scapegoat all this time.
...If someone who bore the Sonozaki name -- even nominally -- were to become romantically involved with a Houjou... it would undermine everything.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ...I did not like that one bit.
...Satoko was friends with Mion at school, was she not?
A friendship between girls from opposing families was apparently fine... but my relationship with Satoshi-kun was not.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ......But... if I did something conspicuous again... this time, it might bring real harm to Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Just as they had forced me to tear out my own fingernails.
...The Sonozaki main family does not bluff when push comes to shove.@k ...I could all too easily imagine what cruelties they might inflict on Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...If I truly loved him... perhaps it was better that I stayed away from him.
Unless Satoshi-kun returned to the Hinamizawa Fighters...@k he was unlikely to come to Okinomiya again anytime soon.
If I wanted to go to Hinamizawa, I would have to impersonate Mion again.
But Mion... would never cooperate with me again.
changeReason: Character name 'Mion' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Even if she eventually forgave me, that would not happen until things had blown over... a long time from now.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: ......Even if they had not explicitly told me not to see Satoshi-kun...
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: I had already lost every means of meeting him again.
Ah... wait.
...Mion had given me his home phone number before.@k ...I could call him.@k ...Maybe I could even ask him to meet me in secret.
.....................Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150601029. I would like... to be alone for a while.
Kasai assumed I wanted him to leave and hastily began cleaning up the meal.
"Oh, no, it is fine.@k@vS06/06/150601031. Take your time and finish eating, Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150601032. I just want to go for a walk by myself."
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...........I cannot lock this room unless I leave with you.
Kasai quickly tidied up and came out the door with me.
...Just so we are clear... I am not going out to see Satoshi-kun.
Understood.
I turned my back to Kasai and started walking.
changeReason: Character name 'Kasai' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: The place I headed, craving solitude, was... a rather pathetic destination.
Yes... it was the toy store that sold the stuffed bear Satoshi-kun had wanted to buy for Satoko.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: I cannot deny that I harbored a faint hope of running into Satoshi-kun there.
Every time someone with a similar build passed by, I could not help peering into their face.
I looked into the toy store's display window.
...That enormous stuffed bear was gone.
changeReason: Old says 'teddy bear' while Japanese (巨大なぬいぐるみ) and new specify 'enormous stuffed bear' — the size detail matters as it's a key plot element.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun......... congratulations.@k@vS06/06/150601035. ...You managed to buy it.
His cruel aunt was dead, and his wretched uncle had vanished to his mistress's house in Okinomiya.
Satoshi-kun had successfully bought the stuffed animal his beloved little sister wanted, fulfilling his duty as her big brother.
There was no one left to torment Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: And the part-time jobs that had chained him down were no longer necessary.
...Satoshi-kun had been freed from all the suffering of those days.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: In time, Satoshi-kun would get his smile back.@k ...And he would return to being the Satoshi-kun I had fallen in love with.
...............But.@k ......I would not be there.
I heard a car horn.
I did not think it was directed at me at first, but it kept honking so persistently that I finally looked up.
Someone was waving from the car.@k ......It was the Coach -- Irie.
Oblivious to what I was feeling, he was waving enthusiastically with that carefree smile of his.
"Sonozaki-san~!@k@vS06/10/151000115. It has been a while!@k@vS06/10/151000116. Oh, could it be you are meeting up with Satoshi-kun?"
changeReason: Character name 'Houjou' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ...No, that is not it.
"Satoshi-kun used to look into that toy store's window all the time, you know.@k@vS06/10/151000118. ...So I just assumed you were waiting for him here or something."
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...No, I was just passing by.@k@vS06/06/150601038. .........It looks like he was able to buy it, though.
The Coach seemed to know about the stuffed bear as well.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: He smiled upon seeing the large empty space where the bear had been.
......Yesterday was Satoko-chan's birthday.@k@vS06/10/151000120. I am sure he was able to celebrate it with that stuffed bear.
"So Satoshi-kun does not have to keep working himself to the bone anymore, right?"
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: That is right.
"......Did Satoshi-kun... you know... submit his resignation to the Hinamizawa Fighters?"
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Has he? I never received anything like that.@k@vS06/10/151000123. Did you, Sonozaki-san -- as the team manager?
...Ahahaha, no, I have not.
If nobody received a resignation, then, haha, he is still a member of our team.@k@vS06/10/151000126. I think he will come back once things have settled down.
But if you are not there when he comes back... he will surely be sad.
The Coach said it with a teasing tone.@k ...He was a grown-up, after all.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: He could tell my spirits were low, and I felt he was deliberately putting on a cheerful front for my sake.
"......Sonozaki-san, please come back to the team whenever you are ready.@k@vS06/10/151000129. It really is not the same without you."
changeReason: Character name 'Sonozaki' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...When you do come back, I have been thinking of redesigning the manager's uniform into a maid outfit.
I could not help but burst out laughing.@k The Coach never changed.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ......Oh, right.@k@vS06/06/150601043. I can use my real name now.@k@vS06/06/150601044. Coach, I am sorry for deceiving you all this time.@k@vS06/06/150601045. ...I am not Mion.@k@vS06/06/150601046. I am her twin sister, Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601047. For various reasons, I have been pretending to be Mion.
Wh-... what?@k@vS06/10/151000132. Mion-san's twin... sister...?!
The Coach's flustered reaction was so amusing that I burst out laughing again.
That is right.@k@vS06/06/150601049. My name is Sonozaki Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601050. The Sonozaki main family does not like me much, so I cannot exactly live out in the open.
...I had no idea...@k@vS06/10/151000134. I never imagined that Mion-san had a twin sister......
Na ha ha ha. I feel exactly the same.
Suddenly, a deep, booming voice cut in.
A greasy, unpleasant laugh -- nothing like the Coach's bright, open one.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ...It was him.@k Ooishi.
Good afternoon, Dr. Irie.@k@vS06/11/150700146. Thank you for your time the other night.
Not at all. I am sorry I could not be of more help.
...The kind of perfunctory pleasantries adults exchange.
It was plain as day that neither of them thought highly of the other.
changeReason: Old says 'they hated each other' while Japanese (良く思いあっていない) and new say 'neither thought highly of the other' — hatred vs mild dislike is a significant difference in characterization.

TextENGNew: The Coach leaned in and whispered to me.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ......I am about to leave. Would you like a ride?@k@vS06/10/151000137. I can take you home.
The Coach had guessed that Ooishi's target was me and was subtly offering me an escape.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Looking at Ooishi's face, it was clear that his interest was aimed squarely at me.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ...He was surely planning to press me about my testimony that I had been with Satoshi-kun on the day of the festival -- the day of the incident -- until I slipped up.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...I was confident I could dodge him, but I had no desire to waste my time on something so unpleasant.
You are right.@k@vS06/06/150601050_2. Well then, Coach,@k@vS06/06/150601050_3. shall we get going?
...Oh, were the two of you on your way somewhere together?
Yes.@k@vS06/10/151000142. We are out shopping for prizes for the team's evening party.@k@vS06/10/151000143. We have several more stores to visit, so if you will excuse us.
The Coach bowed, and I followed suit.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ...The Coach really was an adult.@k He rattled off lies with perfect poise, without a trace of hesitation.
Ooishi seemed to know it was a lie, too.
But he did not have a warrant, so he could not forcibly detain us.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ......Is your injury healing all right?@k@vS06/11/150700149. Shion Sonozaki-san.
I froze mid-step, about to get into the Coach's car.
...My left hand was wrapped in bandages.
...It was unsightly, so I had been keeping it stuffed in my pocket.
There was no way Ooishi could have seen it.
As long as it does not get infected, it will heal up nicely.@k@vS06/11/150700151. I lost a few fingernails myself as a kid. But look -- good as new, right?@k@vS06/11/150700152. Nfu fu fu.
......A man bragging about his pretty nails is just creepy.
Ooishi scratched his head and laughed, agreeing wholeheartedly.
...Ooishi was reputed to be the sharpest detective at the Okinomiya precinct, well-versed in the region's underground dealings, including those in Hinamizawa.
changeReason: Character name 'Ooishi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: I see... so he had caught wind of my punishment from somewhere...
...But what happened that day took place deep within the inner sanctum of the Sonozaki main house.
Whatever his source, this man's information network was not to be underestimated.
I am deeply moved by your concern.@k@vS06/06/150601053. ...Well, we must be going now.@k@vS06/06/150601054. Excuse us.
Shion Sonozaki-san.@k@vS06/11/150700154. Why don't you join me for coffee?
...Excuse me?@k@vS06/06/150601056. Are you asking me out?
If you want to see it that way, sure.@k@vS06/11/150700156. Nfu fu fu.
I will pass.@k@vS06/06/150601058. There are only twenty-four hours in a day.@k@vS06/06/150601059. Wasting them on something tedious would be idiotic.

Ah... you got me there...@k@vS06/11/150700158. ...All right, how about this?@k@vS06/11/150700159. If talking to me gets boring, you are free to leave at any time.@k@vS06/11/150700160. I will not try to stop you.@k@vS06/11/150700161. How does that arrangement sound?
...You can talk all you want, but I will not be answering.@k@vS06/06/150601061. Whether that qualifies as a conversation is debatable.

Yes, yes, that is perfectly fine.@k@vS06/11/150700163. You can just be the listener.@k@vS06/11/150700164. I will simply talk at you one-sidedly.@k@vS06/11/150700165. ...You just sip your coffee and let a middle-aged man's rambling wash over you. That will be enough.
......What a strangely aggressive approach.
Oddly, it did not feel like he was trying to crack my story open.

This man who knew about my fingernails -- what sort of "talk" could he possibly offer that would not bore me?@k .........I was skeptical, but I could not deny that a small spark of curiosity had been lit.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: "...Coach.@k@vS06/06/150601063. I am sorry, but I think I will go on a little date with dear old Ooishi-sama instead."
changeReason: Character name 'Ooishi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Mio... no, Shion-san......?
I winked at him, telling him not to worry.
...The Coach did not press the matter. He got into his car and drove away.
changeReason: Character name 'Irie' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Behind me, Ooishi was backing his car up to where I stood.
I did not want to sit in the front seat, so I climbed into the back.
...Are we really going to a cafe?
Hmm... if you could point me to one with no connection to your family, that would be a great help.
Tch.@k@vS06/06/150601066. ...Then just take me wherever you want.
Ooishi -- more careful than his appearance would suggest -- checked both ways for traffic, used his turn signal, and then pulled out.
True to expectations, Ooishi drove us straight to the Okinomiya police station.
...If he had actually taken me to some stylish cafe, I might have raised my opinion of him a notch.@k ...But of course, that was too much to hope for.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: I thought it would be more convenient to come here.@k@vS06/11/150700168. ...Our agreement still stands, of course. You may leave whenever my talk gets boring.
......Well then, do your best not to bore me.@k@vS06/06/150601068. ...Shall I walk in arm-in-arm with you, dear sir?
Ooishi laughed heartily and got out of the car, then led the way inside...
The room he brought me to was not a cramped interrogation room, but rather an office with "Criminal Investigation Division" written on the door.
...Officers in uniforms and plainclothes detectives were going about their work at their desks, answering phones, or taking it easy.
In front of a slightly grander, unoccupied desk labeled "Section Chief," there were a pair of facing sofas and a faux-marble table.
Ooishi told me to sit there, then walked over to a cabinet and began preparing instant coffee.
I had never been in a place like this inside a police station before, so I was looking around restlessly, taking everything in.
Did you think I was going to sit you down in an interrogation room and feed you pork cutlet on rice?
Ooishi brought over two guest-style coffee cups on a dull silver tray.
...The hospitality is better than I expected.@k@vS06/06/150601070. I was prepared to be kneeling on the courtyard gravel at the very least.
Hmph, that is not my style.@k@vS06/11/150700171. Na ha ha ha ha.
So?@k@vS06/06/150601072. ...Were you not going to regale me with your talk?
"Very well. I will get straight to the point.@k@vS06/11/150700173. Shion-san, are you in love with Houjou Satoshi?"
changeReason: Character name 'Shion' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...........Huh?@k@vS06/06/150601074. ...This is already boring. I am leaving.
Na ha ha ha ha... all right, forget I said that.
...If this man knew about my torn fingernails, then he knew the circumstances that led to it.
changeReason: Character name 'Ooishi' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: That question was not an attempt to confirm whether I liked Satoshi-kun. It was his way of letting me know he already knew.
...I already gave Detective Kumagai a full account of Satoshi-kun's alibi for the day of the incident.
Of course. I have read Kuma-cha... I mean, Kumagai-kun's report.@k@vS06/11/150700176. I know you were with him.
I will give you the same statement no matter how many times you ask.@k@vS06/06/150601077. If you think you can trip me up by asking over and over, you are sorely mistaken.
No, no, I would never think that.@k@vS06/11/150700178. You are a clever person.@k@vS06/11/150700179. I know a trick like that would never work on you.

Then what trick are you planning?@k@vS06/06/150601079. Either way, trying to get information about Satoshi-kun from me is a waste of time.@k@vS06/06/150601080. There is nothing beyond what I already told you.
Ah, what a shame. This is boring after all.@k@vS06/06/150601082. I am going home.@k@vS06/06/150601083. That was our agreement, was it not?@k@vS06/06/150601084. Excuse me.
I said that much and stood up from the sofa.
Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700181. I admit that I did want to ask you about Satoshi-san's alibi.@k@vS06/11/150700182. I will not deny that.
...Then that settles it.@k@vS06/06/150601086. It is boring.@k@vS06/06/150601087. I am leaving.
I turned on my heel, my shoes clicking against the floor, and headed for the exit.
changeReason: Old translation appears significantly truncated compared to new translation and Japanese source.

TextENGNew: Ooishi did not panic, but he raised his voice slightly.
But Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700184. What I want to ask about is not his alibi for the day of the incident.@k@vS06/11/150700185. It is his alibi for yesterday.
Yesterday...? Why would you need his alibi for yesterday?@k@vS06/06/150601089. What does yesterday have to do with his aunt's murder?
Because Satoshi-kun went out yesterday and has not been seen since. He has vanished.
...What?
...You did not know he had disappeared?
I could not bring myself to answer aloud, but my expression must have said everything.

TextENGNew: What do you mean...?@k@vS06/06/150601092. Disappeared... what?
Why don't you sit back down?
Satoshi-kun disappeared, you said...?!@k@vS06/06/150601094. What?!!
The one thing we know for certain is that he withdrew all his savings at the Okinomiya post office before three in the afternoon.@k@vS06/11/150700190. After that, we have lost all trace of him.

TextENGNew: I understood why he had withdrawn all his money.
It was to buy Satoko's birthday present -- that stuffed bear.
...So Satoshi-kun had gone to buy the bear yesterday.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...This is just my speculation, mind you.@k@vS06/11/150700192. But... I wonder if maybe he took that money and skipped town.
You think he used it as getaway money?@k@vS06/06/150601096. That is absurd.@k@vS06/06/150601097. He had been saving that money to buy Satoko a birthday present.
He bought that huge, expensive stuffed bear from the toy store with it.@k@vS06/06/150601099. There is no way he had enough left over to fund some escape.
Yes, the most expensive stuffed animal in that toy store was indeed sold.
But we cannot confirm that it was Satoshi-kun who bought it.
Then just ask the clerk!@k@vS06/06/150601101. Satoshi-kun and I reserved it together!@k@vS06/06/150601102. ......Oh...
A terrible thought crossed my mind.
......It was that old man who had been minding the shop.@k He had looked completely unreliable.
...What if he had been watching the store again that day, and...@k even though Satoshi-kun had bought it, the old man did not even remember selling it...?!
Ooishi put on a wry smile.
...From his expression, it was clear he had already tried questioning that old man himself.
A-Anyway!!@k@vS06/06/150601104. Satoshi-kun is the one who bought it!
Satoshi-kun barely had enough money for the stuffed bear,@k@vS06/06/150601106. so there is no way he could have afforded it and still had funds to flee.@k@vS06/06/150601107. It makes no sense!
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: All right.@k@vS06/11/150700196. Let us say he really did buy the stuffed bear.@k@vS06/11/150700197. Then why did he not bring it home?
At that moment...@k ......I felt something like frost crystallize deep inside my heart.
Satoshi-kun had been pushing both his mind and body to the breaking point, working himself ragged at part-time jobs just to earn enough to buy that stuffed bear.
And then, at last, he had bought it.
It was unthinkable that someone else had coincidentally come along and bought that exact bear on a whim!
The bear was gone because Satoshi-kun had bought it.
That much was absolutely, unquestionably certain!
But if that was true...
...Satoshi-kun would have given that hard-won bear to Satoko. Without a doubt.
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: And yet, despite buying the bear... he had not gone home.
He had not gone home?
That was impossible.
If he had bought the bear, he would have headed straight home without a second thought.
There was no way he would buy the bear and then decide to run off somewhere.
Even if Satoshi-kun had wanted to flee from the police...
He would never, ever have bought the bear and then disappeared without giving it to Satoko.
Anyway, the one who bought that bear was Satoshi-kun!@k That much was absolutely beyond doubt.
The question was -- why had he been unable to bring it home?!
That bear was enormous.
It would never fit in a bicycle's front basket.
At best, he would have had to bring some cord and strap it to the rear rack, wobbling along precariously.
In that state, there was no way he would have thought of stopping somewhere or making a detour.
So he absolutely, without question, would have gone straight from "post office" to "toy store" to "home"!
The path from "post office" to "toy store" was certain.@k It had to be!@k Nobody else could have bought that bear!!!@k It was impossible, impossible, absolutely impossible!
That bear had been sitting in the showcase for ages up until that very day.
It had been in plain sight of every passerby.
And yet, right up until that day, it had sat there unsold!
changeReason: Old translation appears significantly truncated compared to new translation and Japanese source.

TextENGNew: So it was impossible that someone else had coincidentally bought it!!!
Therefore, the path from "post office" to "toy store" was beyond question.
The real problem was the stretch from "toy store" to "home."
There should have been no deviation there, either.
With a stuffed bear that huge, he could not have done anything else.
He would not have felt like making any stops!
So the moment he bought it, he would have headed straight home!!
But... Satoshi-kun was not seen after that.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Then the answer was simple.@k Satoshi-kun had vanished somewhere between the "toy store" and "home."
Vanished?@k What did that even mean...?
The one who killed his aunt was almost certainly Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: And Satoshi-kun lacked my kind of cunning.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: So had the steadily tightening net of the police investigation become more than he could bear?
Even though I had helped him at the scene... had the fact that the police had made contact with him convinced him it was only a matter of time before he was found out?
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: And so Satoshi-kun had decided to run?
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Had he judged it better to go into hiding until things blew over, rather than be arrested??
Impossible, impossible, impossible!!!
It was absolutely impossible that he would have come to that decision "while on his way home from buying a stuffed bear"!!!
Even if such a thought had crossed his mind, it would have come only after giving Satoko her present and fulfilling his duty as her brother.
Satoshi-kun treasured Satoko.
I had thought she was too spoiled -- that indulging her so much only made things worse. But regardless, Satoshi-kun had always tried to be the best possible brother to her.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: So there was absolutely no way he would have disappeared without going home!!
The frost inside my chest was spreading -- tearing through the lining of my heart, peeling it back layer by layer.
If it was "absolutely impossible" that Satoshi-kun had vanished of his own will...
Then the answer was simple.
Satoshi-kun had been made to vanish against his will.
Satoshi-kun was......... eliminated.........?@k@vS06/06/150601109. Aha...... ahahahahahahahahahaha!!
Th-This is ridiculous. This is such a boring story, old man.@k@vS06/06/150601111. Ahahahahahahahahahaha!!
I thought that if I laughed loudly enough, I could sweep away the frost that had spread through my chest.
Believing that, I laughed and laughed.@k On and on.@k And on.
The detectives in the room were all staring at me, wondering what had come over me.
changeReason: Old translation appears significantly truncated compared to new translation and Japanese source.

TextENGNew: They looked at me as though doubting my sanity.
I did not care.@k ...I kept laughing until I choked and started coughing...
Ahahahahahahaha... ahahahaha, huff!@k@vS06/06/150601113. Cough, cough!@k@vS06/06/150601114. Ack!!
...I finally took a sip of my now stone-cold coffee, and the laughing fit ended.
The frost had already pierced through the thin wall of my heart, covered my twisted intestines, and was now threatening to break through the skin of my back.
It was so cold... my fingers trembled and rattled against the coffee cup as I held it.

Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700199. I would like you to hear me out without interrupting.@k@vS06/11/150700200. I am going to share a little theory I have put together.@k@vS06/11/150700201. It is all fabrication, every bit of it.
It might sound so far-fetched that you will want to burst out laughing.@k@vS06/11/150700203. Even so, I want you to hear me out to the end.@k@vS06/11/150700204. ...Can you promise me that?
I-... I will promise anything, just...@k@vS06/06/150601117. h-hurry up and tell me...
changeReason: Old translation omits content present in the Japanese; new translation includes additional clauses/sentences.

TextENGNew: The cold enveloping my entire body had my teeth chattering against each other.
My brain was already packed solid with frost... I had lost the ability to think.
...So... I could not think for myself.@k ...All I could do was hear it from Ooishi's lips.
The worst possible scenario -- one I did not want to arrive at on my own.
But a truth I had to hear.
......The deceased aunt had been abusing both Satoshi-kun and Satoko-san. I am well aware of that.@k@vS06/11/150700206. Satoko-san in particular was singled out relentlessly.
...Given all that, if Satoshi-kun's desire to protect his sister gradually turned into murderous intent toward his aunt...@k@vS06/11/150700208. I think that is a perfectly natural human response.
I should have denied it -- should have insisted that Satoshi-kun did not kill his aunt.
...But what Ooishi was saying was true... it had to be the reality.
I could not bring myself to deny it... and so, as promised, I kept silent.
......What follows is pure fantasy, all right?!@k@vS06/11/150700210. Please do not get angry!@k@vS06/11/150700211. And no interrupting!@k@vS06/11/150700212. Understood?!
I-I know...!@k@vS06/06/150601117. Just keep going...!!
Houjou Satoshi planned and carried out the murder of his aunt.@k@vS06/11/150700214. Of course, we have no evidence, all right?!@k@vS06/11/150700215. This is all my delusion.
I know that!@k@vS06/06/150601119. I am well aware it is your delusion!@k@vS06/06/150601120. So hurry up and continue...!!

Now the subject changes.@k@vS06/11/150700217. ...Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700218. You were forbidden from seeing Satoshi-kun, were you not?@k@vS06/11/150700219. They made it clear -- forget him, never have anything to do with him again.
I believe the real reason was not that they despised the relationship between the two families... but that they could not tolerate a Sonozaki daughter being romantically involved with a murderer.

TextENGNew: ......That was... not an impossible theory.
After all... before my identity as Shion was exposed...
changeReason: Character name 'Mion' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: For the Sonozaki main family, living at the heart of Hinamizawa, it would have been child's play to learn that Satoshi-kun was the killer.
They might have even witnessed the murder itself.
That was when having a Sonozaki romantically involved with a murderer suddenly became intolerable to the main family... and could no longer be ignored.

Also... you are known for being the type who does not give up easily, are you not?@k@vS06/11/150700222. In other words, even if the Sonozaki main family threatened you and tore off your fingernails...@k@vS06/11/150700223. you were still not ready to cut ties with Satoshi-kun, were you?
I could not deny it.
True, I had lost most of my means of getting to Hinamizawa, so my connection to Satoshi-kun had been effectively severed.
But I still knew his phone number, and I had been hoping he would eventually return to the Hinamizawa Fighters.

TextENGNew: "I believe the Sonozaki main family knew that mere threats would never be enough to make you give up Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/11/150700225. No amount of intimidation would work on you."

TextENGNew: So... they would have no choice but to deal with the other party, would they not...?
D-D-Deal with him how?!?!@k@vS06/06/150601122. What do you mean, deal with him?!!!@k@vS06/06/150601123. What?!!@k@vS06/06/150601124. What?!!!!
If threatening me was futile...@k ...then they would deal with Satoshi-kun instead.
Deal with him how?!?!
If they "dealt with" him, would that sever the path from "toy store" to "home"?!
"Toy store" to "home"!!
"Toy store" to X to "home"!!!
What was X?! What was X?!
X was what severed the path.@k But what severed it?!
What could have happened to prevent Satoshi-kun from getting from the "toy store" to "home"?!?!
Why, why, why?!?!@k What was X?!!@k X!!!
I blacked out from hyperventilation and dizziness, and collapsed backward.
...The floor was hard, cold tile.
...And yet I felt no pain. I simply crumpled, softly and suddenly, as though I had become a celluloid doll.
Ooishi and several others rushed over with alarmed faces and helped me up.
Calm down, Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700228. ...It is not certain that Satoshi-kun was killed.

TextENGNew: That would have been... an unimaginably bleak and desolate scene.
Those had been long, grueling days.
...His aunt's torment had ground down his spirit... juggling multiple part-time jobs had ground down his body.
...And the money he had finally scraped together from all that.
Clutching it tight... he had rushed to the toy store for his one and only purpose -- a birthday present for his sister.
And just as he always had, he would have searched the showcase for the bear.
But... the large figure that should have been there was gone.
Satoshi-kun was the optimistic type, though.

TextENGNew: ......And then... what exchange took place inside the store.

Had that unreliable old man not even understood his protests, just staring blankly...?
...No, even without that old man, the result might have been the same.

On June 24th, Showa 57, Houjou Satoshi disappeared.
changeReason: Numerical values differ: old has ['1982'], new has ['57'].

TextENGNew: As a prime suspect in the bludgeoning murder that occurred on the 20th of that month, police treated his disappearance as flight and began searching for him.
Initially, there was a report that a boy believed to be him had boarded a Tokyo-bound bullet train at Nagoya Station, but its veracity remains unknown.
His trail went cold after that.
In truth, not only his whereabouts, but even whether he is alive or dead remains unknown.
